message:
fix(dir,inode,journal,mvcc,core,btrfs): harden recovery paths, WAL writer, and directory operations

Directory (ffs-dir):
- Fix entry removal to only track valid previous entries (inode != 0),
  preventing coalescing into deleted slots
- Add test: remove_entry_coalesces_into_previous_live_entry_after_deleted_slot

Inode (ffs-inode):
- Add bounds check in write_inode() rejecting writes that cross block
  boundaries (corruption guard)
- Fix test to use geo.first_inode instead of hardcoded InodeNumber(1)
- Add test: write_inode_crossing_block_boundary_returns_error

Journal (ffs-journal):
- Change unknown fast-commit tags to trigger full recovery fallback
  instead of silent skip (critical correctness fix)
- Update test: replay_unknown_tags_skipped → replay_unknown_tags_require_fallback

WAL (ffs-mvcc):
- wal_writer: extract validation/encoding/rollback into helpers,
  enhance rollback to reset appends_since_sync, add coalesced write
  verification and test for sync failure rollback
- wal_replay: add FailFast policy for monotonicity violations and
  sentinel values with detailed error messages

Core (ffs-core):
- Switch recovery paths (orphan, superblock read/write) from
  block_device_adapter() to direct_block_device_adapter() to persist
  to image baseline instead of MVCC store

Btrfs (ffs-btrfs):
- Improve send stream error reporting with descriptive InvalidField
  messages instead of zeroed InvalidMagic values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
